WATER RUSHING DOWN MAIN STREET, NUMEROUS SECONDARY STREETS FLOODED, UNDERPASS UNDERWATER, LOW AREA NEAR MILES COMMUNITY COLLEGE UNDERWATER.
LARGE WATER TANKS TORN OUT AND THROWN FROM NEAR FENCE INTO THE FIELD. NUMEROUS TREE LIMBS DOWN. WILL CONTINUE TO ASSESS DAMAGE.

ROOF BLOWN OFF OLD TRAILER HOUSE. WINDOWS BLOWN OUT AND SIDING WITH LOTS OF HOLES IN IT FROM WIND-DRIVEN HAIL. TREES DOWN, BRANCHES STUCK INTO SIDE OF HOUSE. HORSE TRAILER ROLLED 50 TO 70 YARDS INTO NEIGHBORS DAM.

CARTER COUNTY DISPATCH REPORTED AN RV AND VEHICLE BLOWN OFF HIGHWAY 212 BETWEEN MILE MARKERS 138-139. NO INJURIES REPORTED.
TENNIS BALL SIZE HAIL TOOK OUT ALL NORTH-FACING WINDOWS...DESTROYED CAR WINDOWS...AND CAUSED SIGNIFICANT ROOF AND SIDING DAMAGE.
POWER LINES DOWN IN TOWN. ALSO ANOTHER REPORT OF A TREE DOWN ACROSS POWER LINES IN A BACKYARD.
AT LEAST 9 REPORTS OF TREES DOWN ALL OVER TOWN. TREES DOWN ON HOUSES, POWER LINES AND ACROSS ROADS. TIME IS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR AND SPOTTER REPORTS.
A COUPLE OF REPORTS OF TREES DOWN AROUND TOWN. HOMEOWNER HAD A TREE DOWN ON HIS HOUSE, A SECOND TREE TOOK A POWER LINE DOWN ACROSS THE ROADWAY.
ESTIMATED 60 MPH WINDS...LOTS OF TREE BRANCHES DOWNED FROM COTTON WOOD TREES.
WINDS ESTIMATED 65 TO 80 MPH... PICK UP CAMPER WITH HATCH OPEN HAD ROOF RIPPED OFF. DIME TO NICKLE SIZE HAIL ALSO REPORTED.
APPEARS TO BE OUTFLOW FROM DISTANT STORM OR POSSIBLY VIRGA RELATED.

COMBINATION TSTM OUTFLOW AND COLD FRONT. SUSTAINED WIND 46 MPH.
SHERIFF DISPATCH REPORTED AN 18 INCH TREE WAS UPROOTED ON THE WEST SIDE OF BAKER FROM VERY STRONG WINDS. TIN ROOFING MATERIAL WAS RIPPED FROM THE FIREHOUSE. MANY TREE BRANCHES DOWN AROUND TOWN.
NEWSPAPER BUILDING RAIL DOOR BLOWN IN OFF HINGES. 80 FOOT TALL 36 INCH DIAMETER COTTONWOOD TREE BLOWN OVER. TRAILERS OVERTURNED. TREE DAMAGE ALL OVER TOWN WITH POWER LINES DOWN.
METAL ROOF BLOWN OFF SHED AND CARRIED BY THE WIND THROUGH CORRALS. DAMAGE TO CORRALS. DOOR BLOWN OFF ANOTHER SHED.
BROKEN BRANCHES 3-4 INCHES IN DIAMETER. STEEL WHEELBARROW FLIPPED OVER.
